name: Commodity additional details (storage temperature)
pageTitle: Consignment Details - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Description of the goods
heading: Additional details

govukComponents.usage: Section caption 'Description of the goods' above the H1
Groups the Temperature radios; legend 'Temperature' rendered at medium size
Storage temperature selection: Ambient / Chilled / Frozen (stacked, not inline)
Wraps the temperature fieldset (also carries bespoke 'temperature' hook class)
Two submit buttons: 'Save and return to hub' and 'Save and continue'
'Back' link (href='#', id=back-link) — carries a non-standard IPAFFS modifier class
'Cancel and return to hub' link inside a govuk-body paragraph

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Bespoke IPAFFS hook class on the form-group wrapping the temperature radios; likely a JS/CSS selector hook, no visual role observed
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Non-Design-System class but purely additive — carries no styling or behaviour visible in the trace. The new app does not need it.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: None required — drop the bespoke class; use the standard govuk-form-group as-is
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d, action 36 (.temperature resolves to DIV.govuk-form-group temperature)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: back-link--next-to-breadcrumbs modifier on govuk-back-link, with href="#"
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: IPAFFS-specific positioning modifier on the standard Back link; the href='#' plus id=back-link implies JS-driven history navigation rather than a real server route
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Non-standard modifier class and a JS-dependent back link (href='#'). The Design System Back link should point to the actual previous page URL and work without JavaScript.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK Back link component with a real href to the previous page
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d, action 36 (Back)

fields.name: temperature
fields.label: Temperature
fields.conditionalOn: always shown on the commodity additional-details page for the IUU/CHED-P (fish, catch-certificate) journey
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d, action 36 (radio name=temperature; ids productTemperature/-2/-3; values ambient|chilled|frozen; legend 'Temperature'). Options list corroborated complete + static by types/temperature.ts:1-5 ({ambient:'Ambient', frozen:'Frozen', chilled:'Chilled'}) and by the page object locator CommodityAdditionalDetailsPage.ts:6-8 (getByRole('radio',{name:'Ambient'})). In every IUU/CHED-P workflow the page is filled by radioAmbient.check() only — observed value 'ambient' (ched-p-workflows.ts:336, :717, :895; a11y test ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:81, where checkPageAccessibility audits it as 'Commodity Additional Details Page' immediately after selecting Ambient). No test drives 'chilled' or 'frozen', so those two values are inferred-valid from the domain list, never observed. NOTE: this page's temperature radio is driven by the hardcoded radioAmbient locator, NOT by the types/temperature.ts domain constant — that constant is actually wired to a SEPARATE dropdownStorageTemperature control on the 'Lab Tests Commodity Sample' page (ched-p-workflows.ts:1051, :1112 select temperature.ambient), a distinct storage-temperature control later in the CHED-P journey. types/temperature.ts is therefore the shared storage-temperature vocabulary for both controls, and the radio's own trace-observed values (ambient|chilled|frozen) match it exactly. required=true is inferred — client-side required attr was false but the field is a mandatory single-select storage-temperature choice; no test asserts the requirement.

buttonVariants.label: Save and review
buttonVariants.context: Amend / review-return context — variant of the primary submit. The page object exposes btnSaveAndReview ('Save and review') alongside btnSaveAndContinue ('Save and continue'). In the fresh-notification IUU flow the trace rendered 'Save and continue' (+ 'Save and return to hub'); 'Save and review' is the label the primary button takes when the page is entered from a Check-your-answers / review return. Not observed in an IUU trace.
buttonVariants.evidence: page object CommodityAdditionalDetailsPage.ts:22-24 (btnSaveAndReview = getByRole('button',{name:'Save and review'})) and enterGrossWeightAndSaveAndReview (:26-29, CHED-PP only). Fresh-notification buttons 'Save and continue'/'Save and return to hub' confirmed at trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d, action 36.

validationMessages.trigger: submitting without selecting a storage temperature
validationMessages.evidence: No error state for the temperature field was captured in this trace (Errors: 0) and no QA test asserts a temperature validation message (grep of tests/ found none). The mandatory-selection error copy is unknown — needs an error-state trace or a human. Not filled from legacy source (out of scope).

openQuestions: Exact URL and route: the page renders under the consignment 'Consignment Details' flow. The returnUrl hidden field shows the surrounding route is /protected/notifications/{chedReference}/consignment/details?commodityDetailsPage={n}. The storage-temperature step's own path was not directly observed via a Navigate action — confirm the exact URL and whether commodityDetailsPage indexes per-commodity.
Validation copy: no error-state trace was captured and no test asserts a temperature error, so the 'select a temperature' error summary/message text is a gap.

The client-side required attribute is false on all three radios; server-side mandatory enforcement is assumed but not observed, and no test asserts it — confirm the temperature radio is genuinely mandatory for IUU/fish.

Options are a fixed 3-value list (Ambient/Chilled/Frozen), corroborated complete + static by types/temperature.ts and the page object. Tests only ever select Ambient — confirm Chilled/Frozen are both offered and valid for IUU/fish, and whether the choice is ever pre-selected/defaulted.

Variant controls exposed by the page object are OUT of the IUU journey and should NOT be built: (a) 'Feedingstuff' radio group is CHED-D-only (ched-d-workflows.ts:235-236); (b) 'Total gross weight' textbox on this page object is never filled here for CHED-P — gross weight is on the Extended Description page. Confirm the IUU 'Additional details' page carries the Temperature radio and nothing else.

The page object also exposes a 'Save and review' primary-submit variant (amend/review return). Confirm whether the IUU journey needs a review-return path and, if so, that the primary button relabels to 'Save and review' in that context.

There are TWO storage-temperature controls in the CHED-P journey: (a) the Temperature RADIO group on this 'Commodity Additional Details' page, and (b) a separate storage-temperature DROPDOWN on the 'Lab Tests Commodity Sample' page (ched-p-workflows.ts:1051,:1112 select temperature.ambient). Both share the same Ambient/Chilled/Frozen vocabulary (types/temperature.ts). The lab-tests page is likely an inspection/sampling-outcome control rather than importer pre-notification — confirm whether the IUU rebuild needs both, one, or neither, and whether they should be reconciled to a single temperature field.

Journey order corroborated by the CHED-P a11y test (ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:70-83): Commodity Extended Description → Commodity Additional Details → Latest Health Certificate. Gross weight, net weight, number of packages and package type are all gathered on the preceding Extended Description page, leaving Additional Details carrying only the Temperature radio for CHED-P/IUU. Confirm this split is intended for the new IUU service (or whether temperature should fold into the preceding page).
